patch.value: SDK ergonomics: package-safe error base, version negotiation, document PM_CLI_PACKAGE_ROOT

SDK-audit findings to make third-party pm package authoring easy and best-practice. (1) Packages hand-redefine a standalone PmCliError whose name must literally equal 'PmCliError' to match Sentry isExpectedCliErrorEvent (pm-calendar/index.ts ~11) — fragile tribal knowledge. Export a package-safe error base / definePmError helper from @unbrained/pm-cli/sdk. (2) No capability/version negotiation: manifest_version is parsed but there's no pm_min_version/engines gate, so version skew throws deep in a handler instead of failing clean. (3) Bundled packages re-import core internals via an undocumented PM_CLI_PACKAGE_ROOT + dist/sdk/runtime.js, a pattern third parties cannot replicate — either document it as first-party-only or convert one package to import purely from @unbrained/pm-cli/sdk as the canonical example. (4) Reconcile registerOutputService vs registerService('output_format') naming drift between EXTENSIONS.md/SDK.md and actual API. (5) Consider an in-SDK test harness for asserting a registered command's contract.
SDK exports a package-safe error primitive; manifest pm_min_version gate; canonical replicable example package; docs reconciled
